passages.title_ja: じこしょうかい
passages.ja: わたしは アンナです。アメリカから 来ました。今、とうきょうの 大学で 日本語を べんきょうしています。しゅみは えいがを 見ることです。どうぞ よろしく おねがいします。

questions.prompt_ja: アンナさんは どこから 来ましたか。
questions.explanation_en: 'アメリカから 来ました'.
questions.explanation_hi: अनुच्छेद में स्पष्ट उल्लेख: "アメリカから 来ました" (अमेरिका से आई)। अन्य विकल्प (जापान, टोक्यो, चीन) उल्लेखित हैं पर मूल देश नहीं।

questions.prompt_ja: アンナさんは 今 どこで 日本語を べんきょうしていますか。
choices: とうきょうの こうこう
とうきょうの 大学
questions.correctAnswer: とうきょうの 大学
questions.explanation_en: '今、とうきょうの 大学で 日本語を べんきょうしています'.
questions.explanation_hi: अनुच्छेद में: "今、とうきょうの 大学で 日本語を べんきょうしています" (अभी टोक्यो के विश्वविद्यालय में जापानी पढ़ रही हूँ)।

passages.summary: じこしょうかい (self-introduction विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।
passages.cultural_context: どうぞよろしくおねがいします (douzo yoroshiku onegai shimasu, "please treat me well") is the standard self-introduction closer in Japan. Used after stating your name, origin, and what you do. The expected response is こちらこそ、よろしくおねがいします (kochira koso, yoroshiku onegai shimasu — "same here, pleased to meet you"). Mandatory in formal first meetings: business, school, neighbours, parents-in-law. Skipping it reads as cold or rude.

grammar_footnotes.note: は marks "as for me" — the topic of the rest of the discourse.
から here is "from" (origin of motion / location). Pairs with 来ました.
で marks the location-of-action: "study AT the Tokyo university".
〜ています = present continuous / habitual: "is studying / studies".
見ること — verb-plain + こと turns "see" into the noun "seeing / watching", which is then connected with は as the subject of "is my hobby".

passages.summary_hi: अमेरिका से आई एक छात्रा अन्ना अपना परिचय देती है — टोक्यो विश्वविद्यालय में जापानी पढ़ रही है, फ़िल्में देखना उसका शौक़ है।

paragraphs.text_ja: わたしは アンナです。アメリカから 来ました。今、とうきょうの 大学で 日本語を べんきょうしています。しゅみは えいがを 見ることです。どうぞ よろしく おねがいします。

passages.translation_literal: I am Anna. I came from America. Now, in Tokyo's university, I am studying Japanese language. My hobby is watching movies. Please be kind to me.
passages.translation_natural: I'm Anna, from America. I'm currently studying Japanese at a university in Tokyo. My hobby is watching movies. Nice to meet you.

cultural_callout.note: Japanese self-intros follow a fixed sequence: greeting → name → affiliation → "yoroshiku".
School year starts in April; senior/junior (senpai (せんぱい) / kouhai (こうはい)) hierarchy is rigid.
Hobby-asking is a standard small-talk move; vague answers are polite.

cultural_callout.label_en: Manners (ojigi (おじぎ), kutsu wo nugu (くつを ぬぐ))
cultural_callout.note: Bowing depth signals respect level; shoes off indoors; meishi exchange has strict choreography.
Punctual public transport is a cultural touchstone; Suica/PASMO ubiquity.

passages.title_ja: まいにちの せいかつ
passages.ja: わたしは 毎日 6時に おきます。あさごはんを 食べてから、しごとに 行きます。しごとは 9時から 5時までです。うちに かえってから、テレビを 見ます。10時に ねます。

questions.prompt_ja: この人は 何時に おきますか。
questions.explanation_en: '毎日 6時に おきます'.
questions.explanation_hi: दैनिक दिनचर्या में स्पष्ट: "毎日 6時に おきます" (हर दिन 6 बजे उठती हूँ)।

questions.prompt_ja: しごとは 何時から 何時までですか。
questions.explanation_en: 'しごとは 9時から 5時までです'.
questions.explanation_hi: काम का समय अनुच्छेद में दिया गया: "しごとは 9時から 5時までです" (काम 9 से 5 तक है)।

questions.prompt_ja: うちに かえってから 何を しますか。
choices: あさごはんを 食べます
questions.explanation_en: 'うちに かえってから、テレビを 見ます'.
questions.explanation_hi: घर लौटने के बाद की क्रिया: "うちに かえってから、テレビを 見ます" (घर लौटकर टीवी देखती हूँ)।

passages.summary: まいにちの せいかつ (daily routine विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।

grammar_footnotes.note: に marks the specific clock-time: "wake up AT 6:00".
〜てから = "after doing X". Here: "after eating breakfast, I go to work".
〜から〜まで = "from X to Y" (range). Here: "9 to 5".
〜てから again — "after returning home, I watch TV".

passages.summary_hi: लेखक की दिनचर्या — सुबह 6 बजे उठना, नाश्ता, 9-5 की नौकरी, घर लौटकर टीवी, रात 10 बजे सोना।
passages.cultural_context: A 9-to-5 workday in Japan often stretches longer in practice (残業, zangyō, "overtime" is common in white-collar roles), but the polite default written form remains "9 to 5" because overtime is socially expected, not contractually scheduled. Reading "wakes at 6, works 9-5" maps to a typical urban commuting routine.

paragraphs.text_ja: わたしは 毎日 6時に おきます。あさごはんを 食べてから、しごとに 行きます。しごとは 9時から 5時までです。うちに かえってから、テレビを 見ます。10時に ねます。

passages.translation_literal: I, every day, at 6 o'clock, get up. After eating breakfast, I go to work. Work is from 9 o'clock until 5 o'clock. After returning home, I watch TV. At 10 o'clock, I sleep.
passages.translation_natural: I get up at 6 every day, eat breakfast, then head to work. I work from 9 to 5. After getting home I watch TV, and I'm in bed by 10.

cultural_callout.note: Japanese self-intros follow a fixed sequence: greeting → name → affiliation → "yoroshiku".
Itadakimasu/gochisousama bracket meals; opt-out can offend hosts.
Otsukaresama is a workplace closing; honne (ほんね) / tatemae (たてまえ) (true vs public face) shapes office speech.
Washoku is UNESCO-listed; specific food terms carry strong cultural connotation.

cultural_callout.note: Bowing depth signals respect level; shoes off indoors; meishi exchange has strict choreography.

passages.title_ja: しゅうまつの よてい
passages.ja: あした、ともだちと えいがを 見に 行きます。えいがの あとで、レストランで ばんごはんを 食べます。とても たのしみです。

questions.prompt_ja: あした 何を しますか。
choices: うちで 本を よみます
しごとに 行きます
ともだちと えいがを 見ます
questions.correctAnswer: ともだちと えいがを 見ます
questions.explanation_en: 'ともだちと えいがを 見に 行きます'.
questions.explanation_hi: अनुच्छेद से: "ともだちと えいがを 見に 行きます" (दोस्तों के साथ फ़िल्म देखने जाते हैं)।

questions.prompt_ja: えいがの あと、何を しますか。
choices: うちに かえります
ばんごはんを 食べます
questions.correctAnswer: ばんごはんを 食べます
questions.explanation_en: 'えいがの あとで、レストランで ばんごはんを 食べます'.
questions.explanation_hi: फ़िल्म के बाद की योजना: "えいがの あとで、レストランで ばんごはんを 食べます" (फ़िल्म के बाद रेस्टोरेंट में रात का खाना)।

passages.summary: しゅうまつの よてい (weekend plan विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।

grammar_footnotes.note: と here = "with" (companion). "Going with friends".
Verb-stem + に行く = "go to do X". 見に 行く = "go to see (a movie)".
〜のあとで = "after X". Here: "after the movie".
で = location of action: "eat AT the restaurant".

passages.summary_hi: कल दोस्त के साथ फ़िल्म देखने जाना है, फ़िल्म के बाद रेस्तरां में रात का खाना — लेखक उत्साहित है।
passages.cultural_context: えいが (movie) + ばんごはん (dinner) is the canonical Japanese casual outing pairing — equivalent to "dinner and a movie" in English-speaking countries. Movie tickets in Japan are ¥1,800-2,000 standard, with 1,200 yen "Lady's Day" discounts on Wednesdays still common at major chains.

paragraphs.text_ja: あした、ともだちと えいがを 見に 行きます。えいがの あとで、レストランで ばんごはんを 食べます。とても たのしみです。

passages.translation_literal: Tomorrow, with my friend, we are going to see a movie. After the movie, at a restaurant, we will eat dinner. I am very much looking forward to it.
passages.translation_natural: Tomorrow my friend and I are going to a movie, then dinner at a restaurant afterward. I can't wait.

passages.title_ja: コンビニで
passages.ja: きのう、コンビニで パンと コーヒーを 買いました。パンは 200円で、コーヒーは 150円でした。ぜんぶで 350円でした。やすかったです。

questions.prompt_ja: コンビニで 何を 買いましたか。
choices: おかしと ジュース
ごはんと コーヒー
questions.explanation_en: 'パンと コーヒーを 買いました'.
questions.explanation_hi: दुकान से ख़रीद का विवरण: "パンと コーヒーを 買いました" (रोटी और कॉफ़ी ख़रीदी)।

questions.prompt_ja: ぜんぶで いくらでしたか。

passages.summary: コンビニで (shopping विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।

grammar_footnotes.note: で = location of action: "buy AT the konbini".
と = "and" (exhaustive list): "bread AND coffee".
買いました = polite past affirmative ("bought").
〜で = な-adjective て-form connector linking two clauses with the same subject — except here it is connecting two complete clauses with "and" sense.
やすかった = い-adjective past ("was cheap").

passages.summary_hi: कल कन्वीनिएंस स्टोर पर ब्रेड (200 येन) और कॉफ़ी (150 येन) ख़रीदी, कुल 350 येन — सस्ती ख़रीदारी थी।
passages.cultural_context: コンビニ (konbini, "convenience store" — 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, Lawson) are central to Japanese daily life: fresh bento, package pickup, ATM, copying, and bill payment, 24/7. A ¥350 breakfast (pan + coffee) is the standard pre-commute price point.

paragraphs.text_ja: きのう、コンビニで パンと コーヒーを 買いました。パンは 200円で、コーヒーは 150円でした。ぜんぶで 350円でした。やすかったです。

passages.translation_literal: Yesterday, at the convenience store, I bought bread and coffee. The bread was 200 yen, and the coffee was 150 yen. Altogether, it was 350 yen. It was cheap.
passages.translation_natural: Yesterday I bought bread and coffee at the convenience store. The bread was 200 yen, the coffee 150 yen — 350 yen total. Pretty cheap.

passages.title_ja: わたしの かぞく
passages.ja: わたしの かぞくは 4人です。父と 母と あにが います。父は 先生で、母は いしゃです。あには 大学生です。わたしは こうこうせいです。みんな げんきです。

questions.prompt_ja: かぞくは 何人ですか。
questions.explanation_en: 'わたしの かぞくは 4人です'.
questions.explanation_hi: पारिवारिक संख्या स्पष्ट: "わたしの かぞくは 4人です" (मेरा परिवार 4 लोगों का है)।

questions.prompt_ja: 母の しごとは 何ですか。
questions.explanation_en: '母は いしゃです'.
questions.explanation_hi: माँ का व्यवसाय: "母は いしゃです" (माँ डॉक्टर हैं)।

questions.prompt_ja: この人は 何ですか。
questions.explanation_en: 'わたしは こうこうせいです'.
questions.explanation_hi: वक्ता का विद्यार्थी-स्तर: "わたしは こうこうせいです" (मैं हाई-स्कूल का छात्र हूँ)।

passages.summary: わたしの かぞく (family विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।
passages.cultural_context: Japanese family terms split between humble (own family) and respectful (someone else's). 父 (chichi) / 母 (haha) refer to ONE'S OWN parents in humble speech to outsiders. お父さん (otousan) / お母さん (okaasan) refer to someone else's parents — or to your own parents when you are addressing them directly at home. Same pattern: あに / あね (own older brother / sister) vs お兄さん / お姉さん. This own-vs-others split is core to Japanese in-group / out-group politeness (うち / そと).

grammar_footnotes.note: の = possessive: "MY family".
います = exists (animate). Used because family members are people.
で = na/noun-copula て-form: "father IS-a-teacher AND mother IS-a-doctor".
な-adjective + です: げんきです = "are healthy / energetic".

passages.summary_hi: चार लोगों का परिवार — पिता शिक्षक, माता डॉक्टर, बड़ा भाई कॉलेज छात्र, लेखक हाई स्कूल में, सब स्वस्थ।

paragraphs.text_ja: わたしの かぞくは 4人です。父と 母と あにが います。父は 先生で、母は いしゃです。あには 大学生です。わたしは こうこうせいです。みんな げんきです。

passages.translation_literal: My family is 4 people. There are my father, mother, and older brother. My father is a teacher, and my mother is a doctor. My older brother is a university student. I am a high-school student. Everyone is well.
passages.translation_natural: There are four people in my family: my father, mother, and older brother. My father is a teacher and my mother is a doctor. My older brother is in university; I'm in high school. Everyone is doing well.

cultural_callout.note: Japanese self-intros follow a fixed sequence: greeting → name → affiliation → "yoroshiku".
Japanese distinguishes humble (own family: 父/母) vs honorific (others: お父さん/お母さん).
School year starts in April; senior/junior (senpai (せんぱい) / kouhai (こうはい)) hierarchy is rigid.

passages.title_ja: きょうの てんき
passages.ja: 今日は とても さむいです。あさは あめが ふっていました。今は ふっていませんが、まだ さむいです。あした、ゆきが ふると おもいます。

questions.prompt_ja: 今日は どんな 天気ですか。
questions.explanation_en: '今日は とても さむいです'.
questions.explanation_hi: सप्ताहांत की पसंदीदा गतिविधि: "公園で 本を 読みます" (पार्क में किताब पढ़ती हूँ)।

questions.prompt_ja: あさは どうでしたか。
choices: ゆきが ふっていました
あめが ふっていました
questions.correctAnswer: あめが ふっていました
questions.explanation_en: 'あさは あめが ふっていました'.
questions.explanation_hi: सुबह की दिनचर्या से: "朝、コーヒーを 飲みます" (सुबह कॉफ़ी पीती हूँ)।

passages.summary: きょうの てんき (weather विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।

grammar_footnotes.note: い-adjective + です: さむいです = "is cold" (polite).
〜ていました = past progressive: "was raining".
〜ていません = present negative progressive: "is not raining (now)".
が (mid-sentence) = "but" — links two contrasting clauses.
〜と おもいます = "I think that ~" — quotation-marker と + おもう.

passages.summary_hi: आज बहुत ठंडा — सुबह बारिश हो रही थी, अब नहीं हो रही पर ठंड है; कल बर्फ़बारी की आशंका।
passages.cultural_context: Japan's climate varies sharply by latitude — Hokkaido and the Sea-of-Japan side get heavy snow (豪雪, gōsetsu), while Tokyo sees light snow only a few times a winter. The 「雪が ふる」 in this passage suggests the speaker lives in a snow-prone region or expects an unusual Tokyo flurry.

paragraphs.text_ja: 今日は とても さむいです。あさは あめが ふっていました。今は ふっていませんが、まだ さむいです。あした、ゆきが ふると おもいます。

passages.translation_literal: Today is very cold. In the morning, rain was falling. Now it isn't falling, but it is still cold. Tomorrow, I think snow will fall.
passages.translation_natural: It's really cold today. It rained this morning. It isn't raining anymore, but it's still cold. I think it'll snow tomorrow.

passages.title_ja: としょかんの おしらせ
passages.ja:
としょかんの 時間
げつようび 〜 きんようび：9:00 〜 21:00
どようび：10:00 〜 18:00
にちようび：休み
本は 二週間まで かりる ことが できます。

questions.prompt_ja: にちようびに としょかんは あいていますか。
questions.explanation_en: 'にちようび：休み'.

questions.prompt_ja: 本は どのくらい かりる ことが できますか。
questions.explanation_en: '本は 二週間まで かりる ことが できます'.

passages.summary: としょかんの おしらせ (schedule विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।
passages.cultural_context: Public and university libraries (図書館) in Japan typically loan books for 2 weeks (二週間まで), free of charge, with one automatic renewal. Sundays closed (日曜日：休み) is common at school libraries; public libraries usually close on Mondays instead. Online catalogs and reservation systems are universal even at small branches. Quiet-study seating is heavily used.
passages.summary_hi: पुस्तकालय के समय — सोम-शुक्र 9-21, शनिवार 10-18, रविवार बंद; पुस्तक 2 सप्ताह तक उधार।

grammar_footnotes.note: 〜ことができます = "can do (verb)" — ability. Pairs verb-plain + こと + が + できる.
〜ます = polite non-past verb form. Used for present and future actions.

passages.cultural_context: デパート (depaato) — Japanese department stores like Mitsukoshi, Takashimaya, Isetan, Daimaru — are upscale with formal customer service (gift-wrapping, doormen on opening). passages.cultural_context: 桜 (sakura, cherry blossom) blooms in late March to early April depending on latitude — Tokyo around the start of the new fiscal year and the school entrance ceremony (入学式). さくらを見に行く ("going to see sakura") is 花見 (hanami) — picnicking under the trees, often with food and beer. Weather services publish a cherry-blossom front (桜前線) tracking the bloom from Okinawa northward. Sakura is central to Japanese aesthetic identity.

passages.summary: びょうきです (health विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।
passages.cultural_context: 病院 (byouin) in Japanese means both "hospital" and "doctor's clinic" — the same word covers a small neighbourhood clinic and a large hospital. For a cold or headache, you visit a small 内科 (naika, internal medicine clinic) and pay a 30% out-of-pocket fee under the National Health Insurance system; the rest is covered. Same-day walk-ins are standard at clinics. Pharmacies (薬局, yakkyoku) dispense prescriptions next door.

passages.cultural_context: うみに行く (going to the sea / beach) is a summer staple. Japan is an island nation, so most major cities are within 1-2 hours of a beach. Popular destinations from Tokyo: Enoshima, Zushi, Kamakura. From Osaka: Suma. Beach activities are swimming, sunbathing, and BBQ. Beach huts (海の家, umi-no-ie) operate seasonally July-August serving food and shaved ice (かき氷, kakigoori).

passages.cultural_context: Tokyo - Osaka is the most-trafficked corridor in Japan. The 新幹線 (shinkansen, bullet train) is the default choice — about 2.5 hours, runs every 10 minutes, no airport-security overhead. Flights (ひこうき) compete on cost via low-cost carriers (Peach, Jetstar Japan). Tokyo airports: Haneda (HND, closer in) and Narita (NRT, farther). Osaka airports: Itami (ITM, closer in) and Kansai (KIX, on artificial island).

passages.cultural_context: International students (留学生, ryuugakusei) at Japanese universities are predominantly from China, Korea, Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, and Taiwan, with smaller European / American cohorts. The park is quiet and full of flowers in bloom. Drawing is fun, but I'm not very good — I want to practice more. Next month I'm going with a friend to a new museum that supposedly has lots of famous paintings from abroad. I'm looking forward to it.

passages.cultural_context: Visiting the doctor (病院 / clinic) for a cold (かぜ) or fever (ねつ) is normal preventive care in Japan, not just for emergencies. National Health Insurance covers 70% of costs; you pay 30% on the spot. Pharmacies (薬局) are next door to the clinic.

passages.translation_literal: Since yesterday morning, my head was hurting very much. My throat also hurt, and I could not get out of bed quickly. So at 3 PM, I went to the hospital near my house. The doctor said, "You have a fever. It's a cold. Please rest today and tomorrow." I received medicine and went home. I drank hot coffee and went to bed early. Today, my head pain is gone. I am better. Tomorrow, I can go to school.
passages.translation_natural: I had a bad headache from yesterday morning. My throat hurt too, and I couldn't get out of bed easily. So I went to the hospital near my house at 3 PM. The doctor said, "You have a fever. It's a cold. Get some rest today and tomorrow." I got medicine and went home. I had a hot coffee and went to bed early. My headache is gone today — I feel better. I should be able to go to school tomorrow.

passages.summary: らいしゅうの よてい (calendar विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।
passages.cultural_context: Mother's birthday gift culture: in Japan, flowers (花) — typically carnations or roses — and a department-store-wrapped item (デパートでプレゼント) are the formal pattern. Mother's Day (母の日) is the second Sunday of May; carnations are the traditional Mother's Day flower (the colour signals different meanings: red for living mothers, white for remembrance). Birthday gifts overlap with this convention.
passages.summary_hi: अगला सप्ताह व्यस्त — सोम मीटिंग, मंगल/बुध दोस्तों, गुरु माँ का जन्मदिन (फूल + गिफ़्ट), शुक्र-शनि योजनाएँ।

paragraphs.text_ja: らいしゅうは とても いそがしいです。月曜日の あさは、しごとの たいせつな かいぎが あります。火曜日と 水曜日の ばんは、だいがくの ともだちと あいます。木曜日は おかあさんの たんじょうびです。デパートで プレゼントと はなを かいに いきます。金曜日は うちで コンピューターの しごとを します。土曜日と 日曜日は やすみですが、土曜日に せんたくと そうじを ぜんぶ します。日曜日は あさから よるまで うちで ゆっくり ほんを よみたいです。らいしゅうも がんばります。

passages.translation_literal: Next week is very busy. Monday morning there is an important work meeting. Tuesday and Wednesday evenings I will meet university friends. Thursday is my mother's birthday. I will go to the department store to buy a present and flowers. Friday I will do computer work at home. Saturday and Sunday are days off, but on Saturday I will do all the laundry and cleaning. On Sunday, from morning to evening, I want to relax at home and read books. I will do my best next week too.
passages.translation_natural: Next week is going to be hectic. I have an important meeting at work Monday morning. Tuesday and Wednesday evenings I'm meeting up with university friends. Thursday is my mom's birthday — I'll go to the department store for a present and flowers. Friday I'll work from home on the computer. Weekends are off, but I'll do all the laundry and cleaning Saturday. Sunday I just want to relax at home and read all day. Going to give next week my best.

passages.ja: わたしは とうきょうの ぎんこうで はたらいています。まいあさ 八時半に いえを 出て、九時に かいしゃへ いきます。いえから かいしゃまで でんしゃで 三十ぷん かかります。しごとは あさ 九時から ゆうがた 六時までです。ひるごはんは かいしゃの ちかくの しょくどうで どうりょうと たべます。しごとは いそがしいですが、おもしろくて、たのしいです。どうりょうの みなさんは とても しんせつです。しゅうまつは ともだちと あって、おちゃを のんで、いろいろな はなしを します。

passages.summary: わたしの しごと (occupation विषय का छोटा अनुच्छेद)।
passages.cultural_context: Japanese banking (銀行) employees follow a traditional salaryman routine: leave home ~8:30 AM, commute by train (~30 minutes), arrive at the office by 9 AM, paper-based work, lunch at 12-1, work until 6-7 PM. Major banks (メガバンク): Mitsubishi UFJ (三菱UFJ), Sumitomo Mitsui (三井住友), Mizuho (みずほ). Bank teller (窓口担当) is a typical entry-level role; rotation across branches is standard for early-career staff.
passages.summary_hi: टोक्यो के बैंक में लेखक का काम — रोज़ 8:30 घर से, ट्रेन 30 मिनट, 9-6 का काम, बजट के काग़ज़।

passages.translation_literal: I work at a Tokyo bank. Every morning at 8:30 I leave home, and at 9:00 I arrive at the company. From home to company takes 30 minutes by train. Work is from 9 in the morning to 6 in the evening. For lunch, with my colleagues, I eat at the cafeteria near the company. Work is busy, but interesting and fun. My colleagues are all very kind. On weekends, with my friends, I meet up, drink tea, and talk about various things.
passages.translation_natural: I work at a bank in Tokyo. I leave home at 8:30 every morning and get to the office by 9. The commute is 30 minutes by train. I work 9 to 6. I eat lunch with my colleagues at the cafeteria near the office. Work is busy but interesting and enjoyable. My colleagues are all really kind. On weekends I meet up with my friends for tea and lots of conversation.

passages.title_ja: いろいろな 国
passages.ja: 日本の 東に アメリカが あります。日本の 西に 中国が あります。日本には 山も 川も 田も あります。北の 山は ふじ山です。とても 高くて、きれいです。私は 来年 ふじ山に 行きたいです。
passages.translation_literal: To the east of Japan there is America. To the west of Japan there is China. In Japan there are mountains, rivers, and rice fields. The northern mountain is Mt. Fuji. It is very tall and beautiful. I want to go to Mt. Fuji next year.
passages.translation_natural: America lies to the east of Japan; China to the west. Japan has mountains, rivers, and rice paddies. Mt. Fuji is a northern mountain — tall and beautiful. I want to climb it next year.
passages.cultural_context: A short geography passage. Mt. Fuji (ふじ山) is the cultural emblem of Japan, often depicted in art and song.

passages.title_ja: えきの ちかくの みせ
passages.ja: 私の いえの ちかくに、新しい 駅が あります。駅の 出口を 出て、ひだりに あるくと、安い 店が あります。その 店で 入口に 立って いる おじさんは いつも しんせつです。道の むこうにも 大きい 店が あります。そこは 高いですが、いろいろな ものが あります。
passages.translation_literal: Near my house, there is a new station. Coming out of the station's exit, if you walk to the left, there is a cheap shop. The man standing at the entrance of that shop is always kind. Across the road there is also a large shop. It is expensive, but it has various things.
passages.translation_natural: There's a new station near my house. Take the exit, walk left, and you'll see a cheap shop. The man who stands at the entrance there is always nice. Across the road there's a bigger shop too — pricey, but it has lots of things.
passages.cultural_context: Train stations are local landmarks; nearby shops cluster around them.

passages.ja:
ともだちの 山田さんへ
おげんきですか。私は げんきです。

passages.translation_literal:
To my friend Mr. Yamada,
How are you? I am well.
Please tell me your phone number.
There are many things I want to talk about with you.
Thank you for writing me a letter.
From Tanaka

Dear Yamada,
How are you? I'm doing well. Could you give me your phone number? I have a lot to talk about with you. Thanks for writing.
— Tanaka

今日は あさから 雨でした。私は うちの 中で 本を 読んで いました。

passages.translation_literal: Today it was raining from the morning. I was reading a book inside the house. After lunch, the rain stopped. The sky was very beautiful. I went outside and went to the park. In the park many beautiful flowers were blooming. I love spring.
passages.translation_natural: It rained all morning today, so I stayed inside reading. After lunch the rain stopped and the sky cleared up — really pretty. I went out to the park, where lots of flowers were in bloom. Spring is my favorite season.
passages.cultural_context: Cherry-blossom season runs late March to early May; rain-then-sun is the season's signature pattern.

passages.title_ja: 会社の 男の人と 女の人
passages.ja:
私の 会社には、男の 社員が 二十人と、女の 社員が 三十人 います。
中山さんは 大きい 男の人で、力が とても つよいです。
小川さんは 小さい 女の人ですが、とても げんきです。
会社の みんなは いつも 大きい こえで 話します。
いっしょに しごとを するのは たのしいです。
passages.translation_literal: At my company, there are twenty male employees and thirty female employees. Mr. Nakayama is a large man and is very strong. Ms. Ogawa is a small woman, but she is very energetic. Everyone at the company always speaks in a loud voice. It is enjoyable to work together.
passages.translation_natural: My company has twenty men and thirty women on staff. Nakayama is a big guy and very strong. Ogawa is a small woman, but full of energy. Everyone speaks loudly. Working with them is fun.
passages.cultural_context: Japanese companies use 社員 (employees) as a collective term; gender-balanced teams remain less common but improving.

passages.summary_hi: लेखक की कम्पनी में बीस पुरुष और तीस महिला कर्मचारी हैं। नाकायामा बड़े-कद के बलवान हैं, ओगावा छोटी-कद की पर बहुत फुर्तीली। सब लोग ज़ोर से बात करते हैं — साथ काम करना मज़ेदार है।

passages.title_ja: やすい かいもの
passages.ja: きょうの 午前、私は スーパーで かいものを しました。りんごが 一つ 百円で、四つ 買いました。パンは 二百円でした。ぜんぶで 千円ぐらい でした。たくさんの ものが 買えて、うれしかったです。
passages.translation_literal: This morning, I went shopping at the supermarket. Apples were 100 yen each, and I bought four. Bread was 200 yen. In total, it was about 1000 yen. I was happy that I could buy many things.
passages.translation_natural: I went shopping at the supermarket this morning. Apples were 100 yen each, so I got four. Bread was 200 yen. The total came to around 1000 yen. I was glad to get a lot for the money.
passages.cultural_context: Japanese supermarkets often price fruit by individual piece (e.g., りんご 一つ 100円).

passages.title_ja: 友だちとの じゅぎょう
passages.ja: 友だちと いっしょに 学校で べんきょうを します。私たちは 何でも いっしょに します。あさは 上の きょうしつで 日本ごの じゅぎょう、ごごは 下の きょうしつで えいごの じゅぎょう です。まいにち とても たのしいです。
passages.translation_literal: I study with my friend at school. We do everything together. In the morning, we have Japanese class in the upper classroom; in the afternoon, English class in the lower classroom. Every day is very enjoyable.
passages.translation_natural: My friend and I study together at school. We do everything together. We have Japanese class in the upstairs classroom in the morning, and English class in the downstairs classroom in the afternoon. It's a lot of fun.
passages.cultural_context: Schools commonly use 上の/下の for upper/lower floors of a multi-story building.

passages.title_ja: はしった あと
passages.ja: 私は きょう こうえんで 長い 時間 はしりました。いまは 足が とても いたいです。手も つかれました。目も つかれて、なにも 見たくないです。あしたは ゆっくり 休もうと おもいます。
passages.translation_literal: I ran for a long time at the park today. Now my feet hurt very much. My hands are also tired. My eyes are tired too, and I don't want to look at anything. Tomorrow I plan to rest slowly.
passages.translation_natural: I ran for a long time at the park today. Now my feet really hurt, my hands are tired, and my eyes are too tired to look at anything. Tomorrow I'll just take it easy.
passages.cultural_context: Body-part vocabulary: 足 (foot/leg), 手 (hand), 目 (eye). All are basic N5 kanji.

passages.title_ja: みなみへの りょこう
passages.ja: 来月、私は かぞくと 南の しまへ りょこうに 行きます。しまは とても 長くて、うみが きれいです。一万円ぐらいで 行けます。みなみは あつくて、たのしいでしょう。はやく 行きたいです。
passages.translation_literal: Next month, I will travel to a southern island with my family. The island is very long, and the sea is beautiful. We can go for about 10,000 yen. The south will probably be hot and fun. I want to go quickly.
passages.translation_natural: Next month I'm traveling with my family to a southern island. It's a long island and the sea is beautiful. The trip costs about 10,000 yen. The south should be hot and fun — I can't wait.
passages.cultural_context: Many southern islands of Japan (Okinawa region) are popular vacation spots.
